mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, the largest component of aggregate demand. When retail sales are rising month-over-month, corporate revenues and earnings expectations increase, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for ~70% of US GDP
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return in months with rising RSAFS is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or if the rank IC over 3-year windows is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above a threshold of 4.5%, it indicates labor market slack and rising economic distress. This leads to decreased consumer confidence and spending, contracting corporate earnings expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If in periods where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the 5-day forward return is not negative on average (mean return >= 0), or the success rate is below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of the federal funds rate below 1% and year-over-year CPI inflation below 2% signals a highly accommodative monetary policy posture. This environment compresses the risk premium and discount rates, boosting equity valuations. The interaction of low rates and low inflation is more powerful than either signal alone in predicting positive forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Decision time FEDFUNDS=0.37%, CPIAUCSL YoY is moderate
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return in triggered months is not positive and significantly higher than non-triggered months (Welch t-test p < 0.05), or if the interaction term in regression has coefficient with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: The signal from industrial production (INDPRO) is regime-dependent. When INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average (expansion regime), rising production indicates economic strength and leads to positive S&P 500 returns. When INDPRO is below its moving average (contraction regime), further declines signal deepening weakness and produce negative returns. The direction flips sign based on the regime.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return following INDPRO above vs below its MA does not differ significantly (both in same direction) or if the success rate in expansion regime is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) of less than 100,000 indicates a significant labor market slowdown. Such weak job creation reduces income growth expectations and consumer spending, leading to a negative outlook for corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Threshold of 100k is often cited as recession warning
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return in months after payrolls <100k is not negative, or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and real retail sales (RSAFS) are growing at a year-over-year rate above 2% and 4% respectively, it signals a synchronized expansion in both supply and demand. This dual expansion is indicative of a self-reinforcing economic cycle that boosts corporate profits across sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Synchronized expansions historically precede bull markets
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return during synchronized expansion is not significantly positive (average return >0 with p<0.10) or if the success rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The effect of the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) on equity returns depends on the monetary policy regime. During easing cycles (when FEDFUNDS is below its 6-month moving average), lower rates stimulate borrowing and investment, leading to positive returns. During tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS above its MA), higher rates compress valuations, leading to negative returns. The mechanism flips direction between regimes.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns following easing triggers are not statistically significantly higher than following tightening triggers (difference in means test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeds an annualized rate of 3%, it signals above-trend economic expansion. Strong GDP growth boosts corporate earnings expectations and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days).
evidence_refs: fact:Decision date GDPC1 shows strong growth
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return following quarters with GDP growth >3% is not positive on average, or if the success rate is below 55%.
